skill.name: p-video
skill.description: Generate video prompts and inference.sh CLI commands for Pruna P-Video, WAN text-to-video, and WAN image-to-video models.
skill.summary: Create Pruna video generation commands for text, image, and audio workflows.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs users to run infsh commands that submit prompts and media references to a remote inference service. The hardcoded URL findings are mostly documentation, image, and placeholder media URLs, and the weak cryptography blocker at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive with no cryptographic code present.

medium_findings.title: Remote Inference CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill provides infsh app run examples that submit prompts and optional media URLs to Pruna applications through inference.sh. This is expected for the skill, but it can send user content to an external service and may create usage costs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands directly call remote inference applications through infsh. This is a legitimate purpose for the skill, but the external execution and data submission risk is clear.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs Are Mostly Documentation or Placeholders
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh documentation, a hosted preview image, or placeholder media URLs used in examples. No evidence found that the skill exfiltrates credentials or silently contacts unrelated endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in documentation and examples, not hidden network calls. Placeholder media URLs still indicate expected external fetching, so the concern is minor rather than absent.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The static blocker points to the description line, but that line only describes Pruna model optimization and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or encryption code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata text about video generation. There is no cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm usage to evaluate.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill is built around Bash examples for infsh and npx commands. The infsh commands are allowed by the skill metadata, while npx appears only as installation guidance.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and user-visible, so the pattern is real. The risk is moderated because there is no hidden shell script or dynamic command construction.

content.value_statement: Video teams need repeatable commands for fast AI video generation. This skill turns text, image, and audio ideas into Pruna inference.sh workflows.
actual_capabilities: Shows inference.sh commands for Pruna P-Video, WAN-T2V, and WAN-I2V models.
Supports text-to-video examples with duration and resolution options.
Supports image-to-video examples using a prompt and source image URL.
Documents audio input for P-Video workflows that need synchronized sound.
Includes draft mode, 720p, 1080p, and Pruna app discovery commands.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account before use.
Runs remote inference jobs, so availability, latency, and pricing can change.
Media URL examples require externally reachable image or audio files.
Does not provide local video rendering or direct model weights.

use_cases.title: Create Social Video Concepts
use_cases.description: Generate short visual drafts from prompts for campaign testing and creative review.

use_cases.title: Animate Product Images
use_cases.description: Turn static product or portrait images into simple motion clips using WAN image-to-video commands.

use_cases.title: Prototype Video Pipelines
use_cases.description: Combine text, image, audio, resolution, and draft settings into repeatable inference.sh command patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text Video
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a simple P-Video command.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an inference.sh command for Pruna P-Video that generates a five second 720p video from this prompt: [describe scene].

prompt_templates.title: Image Animation
prompt_templates.scenario: Use an existing image as the starting point.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a WAN-I2V command that animates this image URL with subtle natural motion: [image URL]. Use this motion direction: [motion].

prompt_templates.title: Draft And Final Versions
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare a quick draft with a higher quality render.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare two Pruna P-Video commands for this concept: [concept]. One should use draft mode, and one should use 1080p output.

prompt_templates.title: Multimedia Video Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow using prompt, image, and audio inputs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an inference.sh workflow for a video using this scene prompt: [prompt], this image URL: [image URL], and this audio URL: [audio URL]. Include model choice and settings.

output_examples.input: A creator asks for a short beach sunset video in 720p.
output_examples.output: The skill provides a Pruna P-Video command plan with a concise scene prompt, five second duration, and 720p resolution.

output_examples.input: A designer wants to animate a portrait image with gentle motion.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends WAN-I2V and describes the needed image URL, motion prompt, and resolution setting.

output_examples.input: A developer wants to compare cost and quality before final rendering.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines a draft render first, then a higher resolution P-Video render after the concept is approved.

best_practices: Use draft mode for early concept review before running higher resolution jobs.
Use clear prompts that describe subject, motion, camera behavior, and visual style.
Confirm pricing and content permissions before submitting media to remote inference.
anti_patterns: Do not submit private images, audio, or prompts without approval for external processing.
Do not use placeholder media URLs in production commands.
Do not render high resolution outputs before validating the prompt with a cheaper draft.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates inference.sh CLI guidance for Pruna video models, including text-to-video and image-to-video workflows.

faq.question: Which models does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Pruna P-Video, WAN-T2V, and WAN-I2V as documented in the skill file.

faq.question: Does it run video models locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses inference.sh CLI commands that send jobs to remote hosted applications.

faq.question: Can it use audio?
faq.answer: Yes. The P-Video example includes an audio URL field for synchronized video generation.

faq.question: Is an inference.sh account required?
faq.answer: Yes. The quick start requires infsh login before running app commands.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing results?
faq.answer: Check usage cost, media rights, prompt safety, and whether uploaded media may be processed by a third party.
